skill.name: lark-skill-maker
skill.description: Create custom lark-cli Skills. Use when you need to encapsulate Feishu API operations into reusable Skills.
skill.summary: A skill maker template for creating lark-cli Skills that wrap Feishu OpenAPI operations.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external_commands and filesystem patterns, but all findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file is pure documentation containing markdown code blocks (bash examples) and relative links, not executable code. No actual command execution, file system access, or cryptographic operations exist.

content.value_statement: Developers need to encapsulate Feishu API operations into reusable automation. This skill teaches AI to build lark-cli Skills that wrap native APIs or orchestrate multi-step workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through Feishu API discovery using lark-cli schema commands
Create SKILL.md templates with proper metadata and command examples
Orchestrate multi-step API workflows with data passing between steps
Document required OAuth scopes and authentication procedures
Generate dry-run patterns for safe write operations
limitations: Requires lark-cli to be installed and configured
Only works with Feishu/Lark OpenAPI endpoints
Does not execute commands directly - provides guidance only
User must handle authentication and credential management

use_cases.title: Build a room booking Skill
use_cases.description: Create a reusable skill that books meeting rooms via Feishu VC API with automatic conflict detection

use_cases.title: Create user onboarding automation
use_cases.description: Build a skill that provisions new employees across multiple Feishu services in one workflow

use_cases.title: Develop custom reporting tools
use_cases.description: Encapsulate recurring data extraction and report generation into executable skills
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts needing scheduled Feishu data exports

prompt_templates.title: Basic Skill Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to create a lark-cli skill that [describe action]. Help me write the SKILL.md with the correct lark-cli command syntax and required parameters.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner creating their first simple skill

prompt_templates.title: API Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to [goal] but don't know which Feishu API to use. Guide me through using lark-cli schema and api commands to discover the right endpoint.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Step Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a skill that orchestrates these steps: [step 1], [step 2], [step 3]. Show how to pass data between steps and handle errors.

prompt_templates.title: Production-Ready Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete SKILL.md for [feature] including authentication setup, dry-run mode, error handling, rollback procedures, and required OAuth scopes documentation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user preparing skill for team use

output_examples.input: Create a skill to search and book meeting rooms
output_examples.output: A SKILL.md template with lark-cli api POST commands for room search, availability check, and booking confirmation, including required scope: vc:room:read and vc:room:write

output_examples.input: How do I handle authentication in my skill?
output_examples.output: Documentation showing lark-cli auth login --domain <workspace> command placement and scope verification steps before API calls

best_practices: Always include --dry-run preview for skills that modify data
Document exact OAuth scopes required in the permissions table
Use lark-cli schema command to verify parameter definitions before writing skills
anti_patterns: Hardcoding credentials or tokens in SKILL.md files
Skipping confirmation steps for destructive operations like delete
Assuming API responses match expected format without error handling

faq.question: What is lark-cli and how do I install it?
faq.answer: lark-cli is a command-line tool for interacting with Feishu OpenAPI. Install via npm or follow the official installation guide in the larksuite/cli repository.

faq.question: How do I find which API endpoint I need?
faq.answer: Use lark-cli <service> --help to list available resources, then lark-cli schema <service.resource.method> to view parameter definitions. For unregistered APIs, use lark-cli api with the official OpenAPI documentation.

faq.question: Where should I place my custom SKILL.md file?
faq.answer: Custom skills go in skills/lark-<name>/SKILL.md following the naming convention. Reference lark-shared/SKILL.md for common patterns.

faq.question: How do I handle authentication in my skill?
faq.answer: Include lark-cli auth login --domain <workspace-name> in your skill instructions. Document required OAuth scopes in the permissions table of your SKILL.md.

faq.question: Can I create skills that call multiple APIs in sequence?
faq.answer: Yes. Document the step-by-step workflow in your SKILL.md, showing how to capture IDs from step 1 responses and pass them to step 2 requests. Include error handling and rollback procedures.

faq.question: What is the difference between Shortcut and registered API commands?
faq.answer: Shortcuts (lark-cli <service> +<verb>) are high-level abstractions. Registered APIs (lark-cli <service> <resource> <method>) map to specific endpoints. Shortcuts take priority, then registered APIs, then raw api calls.
